title: Cryptantha microphylla
taxonomy: Cryptantha microphylla is classified as a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It belongs to the genus Cryptantha, which comprises over 100 species of small, delicate plants. Cryptantha microphylla is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This species is further classified as a member of the section Microphyllae within the genus Cryptantha. Its classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Boraginales, Family: Boraginaceae, Genus: Cryptantha, Species: C. microphylla.
uses: Cryptantha microphylla is a plant that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues and inflammation. Traditional uses of the plant include its application in treating stomach problems, such as diarrhea and indigestion. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Cryptantha microphylla as a medicinal treatment. Further investigation is necessary to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal uses.

intro: Cryptantha microphylla, also known as small-flowered cryptantha, is a small, annual herbaceous plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Cryptantha microphylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cryptantha microphylla is often prepared as a tincture or infused in hot water as a tea, although it is not commonly cultivated or used in commercial products.
